gazette: The Official Gazette dated April 20, 2023, and numbered 32169
title: Regulation on Amending the Regulation on Duty-Free Shops
bodyHtml:
The Regulation on Amending the Regulation on Duty-Free Shops has been published and entered into force.

Click here to view the regulation.

The prominent changes made are as follows;

Limited or joint-stock companies that have been in operation for at least two years, with a total paid-up capital and reserves of at least 5,000,000 (five million) Turkish Liras and an average of corporate tax paid in the last five years of at least 300,000 (three hundred thousand) Turkish Liras, can apply to open a duty-free shop. For companies with a total paid-up capital and reserves of at least 50,000,000 (fifty million) Turkish Liras, the requirement of two years of operation and corporate tax is not required. (The new capital requirement will apply for applications made after April 20, 2023.)

For foreign individuals residing abroad who are among the board of directors and shareholders who own more than 10 percent of the company's capital, no criminal record certificate is required, and their written statements will be accepted.

The requirements and evaluation process for pre-permit applications have been revised.

Requests for changes in the type of store from a shop to a warehouse or from a warehouse to a shop will be evaluated as a new store or warehouse opening request.

The provision stating that the duty-free shop and warehouse opening and operation permits will be valid for five years from the date of issuance has been repealed. (Repealed provision: The permits for opening and operating duty-free shops and warehouses are issued for a period of five years from the date of issuance of the opening and operating permit document. Shop operators who wish to continue their activities after the expiration of this period shall submit to the customs directorate to which they are affiliated the documents indicating that the conditions stipulated in the relevant legislation for the opening and operation permit are met and request an extension of the validity period of the opening and operation permit. Such requests shall be made no earlier than four months and no later than one month before the expiration of the permit. If the request is made later than one month after the expiration date, entry of goods into the shop and/or warehouse will be suspended for the duration of the delay. The request, together with the views of the customs and regional directorates, shall be forwarded to the Ministry for final evaluation. If the request is found to be appropriate, the validity period of the permit document shall be extended for another five years. The opening and operation permits of shops and warehouses that do not fulfill the conditions stipulated in the relevant legislation at the end of the period shall be cancelled. In the event that the lease agreement between the shop operator firm and the border gate operator is longer than five years, the duration of the duty-free shop/warehouse permit shall be determined by the duration of the lease agreement ).

The provision "domestic goods carried by passengers, the quantity and nature of which are determined by the Ministry" has been removed from the list of goods that can be sold to passengers leaving Turkey, transit passengers, and transport vehicle drivers and service personnel at duty-free shops.

Duty-free shops can sell goods that have not entered into free circulation primarily. If goods that have entered into free circulation are sold in the duty-free shops, the sale will be subject to taxes and duties.

The items listed in Annex-5 can be sold from sales stores on ships.

If payment is desired to be made by credit card, the requirement for the credit card to belong to the passenger making the purchase or another passenger accompanying the purchaser has been removed.

The shipment of goods from the company's warehouse to a store under the supervision of the same customs directorate will only be carried out by making an electronic notification to the customs directorate. At the time of the goods' release from the warehouse, an authorized customs consultant will perform an identification process. After the goods are received at the store under the supervision of the customs or customs surveillance officer designated by the customs directorate, the transaction will be completed by obtaining the necessary approval electronically from the customs directorate. Additionally, necessary corrections will be made in the store and warehouse inventory records by the operator.

The return of goods from the company's store to its warehouse under the supervision of the same customs directorate will be carried out by making an electronic notification to the customs directorate. At the time of the goods' release from the store, the customs directorate will provide the necessary approval electronically. The goods will be transported from the store to the warehouse under the supervision of the customs or customs surveillance officer designated by the customs directorate. An authorized customs consultant will perform an identification process upon the goods' entry to the warehouse. Additionally, necessary corrections will be made in the store and warehouse inventory records by the operator.

The shipment of goods from one store of a company under the supervision of the same customs directorate to another will be carried out by making an electronic notification to the customs directorate in accordance with Article 16. At the time of the goods' release from the store, the customs directorate will provide electronic approval. The goods will be transported from one store to another under the supervision of the customs or customs surveillance officer designated by the customs directorate. After the goods are received at the store, the transaction will be completed by obtaining the necessary approval electronically from the customs directorate. Additionally, necessary corrections will be made in the store's inventory records by the operator.

The lump-sum guarantee amount to be taken per store and warehouse will be 5,000,000 TL for stores selling tobacco, tobacco products, and alcoholic beverages, and 2,000,000 TL for others. After the acceptance or refund of the guarantee letter by the customs directorate, the General Directorate will be informed.

To benefit from the lump-sum guarantee system for multiple stores and warehouses, the required guarantee amount will be updated to 30,000,000 TL through the Electronic Guarantee Module.

Free-shop operators must complete the newly determined guarantee amounts within 90 days (until July 19, 2023) at the latest.
